1_pbx_sizing.principle: PBX model must support required users and concurrent calls with growth margin
1_pbx_sizing.sizing_rule: Users = total phone extensions needed (include soft phones, conference rooms, common areas). Concurrent calls = peak simultaneous calls (typically 15-25% of total users). Add 30% growth margin.

2_module_planning.principle: Telephony modules add analog/digital line connectivity to PBX

O2.function: 2× FXO ports — connects 2 analog PSTN lines
O2.use: Keep existing analog phone lines, failover to PSTN

S2.use: Legacy analog phones, fax machines, analog door intercom, DSPPA PA2S paging adapter

EX08.function: Expansion board with 4 module slots (8 ports)

examples.note: EX30 not in current price list — quote separately

3_sip_trunk_vs_pstn.principle: SIP trunks provide lines via internet; PSTN via analog/digital copper

comparison.capacity: Unlimited lines (concurrent call limited by PBX)

3_sip_trunk_vs_pstn.recommendation: Use SIP trunk as primary (cost effective, scalable). Keep 2-4 analog PSTN lines via O2 module as failover for when internet is down.

4_fanvil_phone_integration.principle: Yeastar PBX auto-provisions Fanvil phones for zero-touch deployment

auto_provisioning.method: DHCP Option 66 or manual provisioning URL

4_fanvil_phone_integration.door_phone_integration: Register Fanvil i-series door phone as SIP extension on Yeastar. Configure inbound rule to ring reception/guard extensions. Door relay triggered via DTMF from phone during call.

5_gateway_selection.principle: Gateways bridge analog PSTN lines to SIP network externally
5_gateway_selection.when_to_use: When PBX has no module slots available, or PSTN lines are in different location from PBX, or using non-Yeastar PBX

TA410.use: 4 analog PSTN lines → SIP trunk to PBX

TA810.use: 8 analog PSTN lines → SIP trunk to PBX

5_gateway_selection.vs_modules: O2 modules are cheaper per line ($32.50/line) vs TA410 ($45/line), but require PBX module slot. Gateways are external and location-independent.

6_call_center_features.principle: P-Series includes built-in call center features without additional licenses
features: Call recording (requires HDD on P560/P570 or USB on P550)

7_hotel_pms_integration.principle: Yeastar P-Series integrates with hotel PMS for room management
7_hotel_pms_integration.compatible_pms: Opera, Protel, Hotelogix, and others via FIAS/TAPI protocol
7_hotel_pms_integration.phone_recommendation: Fanvil H-series (H2U for bathroom, H3/H4 for standard room, H5/H6 for suite, H603W for reception)

8_multi_site_interconnect.principle: Multiple Yeastar PBXs can interconnect via SIP trunk for inter-office calling
8_multi_site_interconnect.method: Create peer SIP trunk between PBXs using internal IP or FQDN over VPN
8_multi_site_interconnect.example: HQ (P570) ←SIP trunk over VPN→ Branch (P550). Extension 1xxx = HQ, 2xxx = Branch. Dial directly.
8_multi_site_interconnect.requirement: Site-to-site VPN (IPSec/OpenVPN) recommended for security and quality.

9_paging_integration_with_dsppa.principle: Yeastar PBX paging reaches DSPPA speakers via multiple paths

methods.method: SIP Multicast Paging → Fanvil A-series/CS speakers
methods.path: PBX → SIP multicast → Fanvil speakers (PoE, self-amplified)
methods.best_for: Small deployments, per-room paging, no existing PA

methods.method: SIP → Fanvil PA2S → DSPPA Amplifier → 100V Speakers
methods.path: PBX → SIP page → PA2S (line out) → DSPPA MP/MA amplifier AUX input → ceiling/horn speakers
methods.best_for: Integrating VoIP paging with existing traditional PA system

methods.path: PBX S2 module FXS → DSPPA amplifier mic/aux input (with impedance matching)
methods.best_for: Simple direct connection, analog audio quality

methods.method: Yeastar PBX → SIP trunk → DSPPA MAG808 IP PA
methods.path: PBX SIP trunk → MAG808 server → MAG network terminals
methods.best_for: Large campus with both VoIP and IP PA systems

standards.scope: Call recording and data privacy compliance
